title: A Princess Talks About a Bad Friend
summary: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit spoke on TV about meeting Jeffrey Epstein, a man who did bad things to people. She said she feels tricked and wishes she never met him. Seven weeks ago, people found out she sent many emails to Epstein from 2011 to 2014 and stayed at his house in Florida. She apologized and said she made poor choices by not checking his background. The princess explained she did not know Epstein was a sex offender, even though information about his crimes was available online. She felt angry that his victims have not gotten justice. During the interview, she was with her husband and said Epstein was a private friend, so she did not tell others about him. She mentioned feeling uneasy once at his house but did not say why. This happened because there was pressure from the prime minister and others for her to explain.

keywords.explanation: when someone makes you believe something that is not true
fair treatment for people who were hurt
a talk where someone asks questions

What did the princess say about meeting Epstein?
options: She wishes she never met him
She wants to see him again

What did the princess apologize for?
Who was with the princess during the interview?
Why did the princess feel uneasy at Epstein's house?

background_read: Jeffrey Epstein was a wealthy man in the US who was convicted for crimes involving underage girls. He died in jail in 2019. Royal families, like in Norway, have members who are public figures and are expected to behave well. Sometimes, people can be tricked by others who seem nice but are actually bad, and it is important to check facts about friends to stay safe.
Article_Structure: WHO: Crown Princess Mette-Marit of Norway
WHAT: She talked about being tricked by Jeffrey Epstein and apologized for not checking his background
WHERE: In Norway on TV and at Epstein's house in Florida
WHY: To explain herself after people found out about her emails and stay with Epstein

perspectives.description: She feels manipulated and deceived, wishes she never met Epstein, and apologizes for poor judgment.
